question_text: Two circuits are set up. Circuit A has three 6 Ω resistors in series connected to a 12 V power supply. Circuit B has three 6 Ω resistors in parallel connected to the same 12 V power supply. Calculate the total current drawn from the power supply in each circuit and explain which circuit draws more current and why.
correct_answer: Circuit A: Total resistance = 18 Ω, Current = 0.67 A. Circuit B: Total resistance = 2 Ω, Current = 6 A. Circuit B draws more current because parallel resistors have a lower total resistance than series resistors.
explanation: In series, resistances add (6 + 6 + 6 = 18 Ω), so current = V/R = 12/18 = 0.67 A. In parallel, 1/R_total = 1/6 + 1/6 + 1/6, giving R_total = 2 Ω, so current = 12/2 = 6 A. The parallel circuit draws more current because the total resistance is lower.
